step.title: Step 43

When calling `python`, emit exactly the tool-call format shown elsewhere in this prompt for this model. sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
Looking at the image, the 3x3 grid now has green cells at:
- Top-right
- Middle-left
- Bottom-right
This matches the purple dot pattern in the left panel! But the level is not completed yet (level_completed: false).
So the pattern is correct, but something else is needed to complete the level. Maybe I need to click on the bar to "confirm" the pattern, or move the cursor to a specific position.
Let me check the bar's cursor position. The cursor is currently at some position in the right section. Maybe I need to move it to the left section (the blue frame) to confirm.
Actually, looking at the image, the cursor is at the far right of the bar's right section. Let me try moving it to the left section and clicking.
Wait, but the bar's left section is a separate element. The cursor is in the right section, and I can move it with LEFT/RIGHT/UP/DOWN keys.
Let me try moving the cursor all the way to the left and see what happens.
Actually, let me first check the current cursor position.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Check current cursor position and 3x3 grid
curr_lines = current_frame.ascii.split('\n')
print("Bar (rows 17-22, cols 10-45):")
for r in range(17, 23):
    print(f"  {r}: '{curr_lines[r][10:45]}'")

print("\n3x3 grid:")
for r in [50, 55, 60]:
    for c in [26, 30, 34]:
        cell = curr_lines[r][c-1:c+2]
        print(f"  ({r}, {c}): '{cell}'")

# The pattern matches the left panel. Maybe I need to click the bar to confirm.
# Let me try clicking on the left section of the bar (blue frame)
action([{'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 19, 'col': 14}])
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
